pass_context.rf10_definition: RF-10 flags: stonewalling, ignoring reasonable requests, unilateral decisions, or refusing to engage in cooperative parenting. CRITICAL NOTE: RF-10 manifests as INACTION — silence and non-response are the primary signals. Keyword hits will be low; structural gap analysis (unanswered messages, response latency, selective responsiveness) is the primary detection methodology.

cross_rf_foundation.rf02_context: RF-02 complete (101 flags). RF-02 is access DENIAL; RF-10 is engagement REFUSAL. These overlap but are distinct: RF-02 = 'you can't see Olivia'; RF-10 = 'I won't engage with your co-parenting requests.' Both can appear in the same event. Required-intermediary arc (64 signals) in RF-02 reflects a co-parenting structure where Melissa routes communication through conditions — that is also RF-10 selective responsiveness.
cross_rf_foundation.rf08_context: RF-08 complete (11 flags, ROW-6819 coaching admission). Information hoarding appears in RF-08 context — Melissa mines Olivia for information about Eli's household while the RF-08 record suggests information sharing to Eli was selective. ROW-6819: 'I remind her of some of the things she's told me' — this is information-gathering FROM Olivia, not information-sharing TO Eli.
cross_rf_foundation.rf09_context: RF-09 XREF-001 (ROW-9964 'There's no threats' vs. ROW-9957 documented threat) — strategic denial is part of selective responsiveness. Melissa responds to messages that suit her narrative and ignores those that don't.
known_signal_areas: Structural gap analysis required across all 31 batches — look for unanswered co-parenting requests
Unilateral decisions: medical (allergy specialist without notice), activity enrollment, school decisions
Information blackout: school information, medical appointments, schedule details
Phase B/C: Eli holding communication boundaries (RF-13 Q5) — Melissa's selective responsiveness may intensify
cross_rf_foundation.methodology_note: Only 2 keyword hits in triage — upgrade to full pass per triage recommendation. RF-10 is about the absence of response. Track: (1) requests that were never answered; (2) unilateral decisions documented in the record; (3) information that was withheld despite a reasonable expectation of sharing. Cross-check against RF-02 records for access-related unilateral decisions.

anticipated_arcs: ARC-RF10-001: Co-Parenting Refusal Pattern — selective responsiveness, stonewalling co-parenting requests
ARC-RF10-002: Information Blackout Pattern — deliberate withholding of medical/school/activity information
not_this_boundaries: Slow response during work hours or reasonable timeframes
Parallel parenting recommended by therapist or court
Setting communication limits around hostile exchanges

calibration_episode.batch_tone: Cooperative baseline Jan-Oct 2022. Co-parenting engagement expected to be functional in pre-escalation period. Very low triage keyword signal (2 hits total across dataset, 0 in Phase B/C) — primary analysis is structural (unanswered messages) rather than content-based.
calibration_episode.reason_not_flagged: Jan-Oct 2022 cooperative baseline. Both triage keyword hits (2 total) appear in later Phase A (Feb 2023 and Aug 2024 hot periods per triage). Zero RF-10 structural signals expected in B-001 cooperative period. Confirming zero primary flags. B-001 establishes the cooperative co-parenting engagement baseline for comparison with Phase A/B/C deterioration.

batch_summary.note: Jan-Oct 2022 calibration batch. Zero RF-10 primary flags. Cooperative baseline — functional co-parenting engagement in pre-escalation period. Primary analysis methodology is structural gap analysis, not keyword detection. Full pass required across all 31 batches to map responsiveness patterns. Arc ARC-RF10-001 and ARC-RF10-002 initialized at CANDIDATE.

arc_updates.event: Co-Parenting Refusal arc initialized — B-001 baseline: functional co-parenting engagement in cooperative period
Information Blackout arc initialized — B-001 baseline: no information withholding documented in cooperative period
